intro.title: Against the Norman
intro.body: Alexios I Komnenos faced Robert Guiscard's Norman invasion of the Adriatic in his first year as emperor. Anna's Alexiad IV-VI gives the daughter's account of her father's campaign, from the loss at Dyrrachium to the recovery of Kastoria.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Alexios crowned 1 April 1081. By autumn the Norman invasion forces him west.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Imperial coronation in Hagia Sophia, banners and lances behind.

stops.name: Thessalonica
stops.note: Alexios musters Varangians and Macedonian levies here, October 1081.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Macedonian capital with Norse axemen and Greek heavy cavalry assembling in the forum.

stops.name: Dyrrachium
stops.note: 18 October 1081. Robert Guiscard breaks the Byzantine army; Alexios is wounded and escapes the field (Alexiad IV.6-7).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Coastal walls under Norman siege, Byzantine cavalry retreating along a rain-soaked track.

stops.name: Kastoria
stops.note: Lake town recovered by Alexios in winter 1082. Anna describes the icy ride across the lake to the Norman-held island.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Frozen lake with island fortress, byzantine cavalry crossing the ice with planks.

stops.name: Larissa
stops.note: Bohemond besieges Larissa 1082-1083. Alexios relieves it; the Normans retreat from Greece.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Thessalian plain town with citadel, Byzantine relief column descending from the pass.
